# Hofuf Formation

**Summary**: Geological formation studied in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]] studied the Hofuf Formation

- [[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013]] studied the hofuf-formation to characterize its sedimentary properties and subsurface structure.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] part of the sedimentary sequence in the eastern part of Saudi Arabia.

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] studied the Hofuf Formation to analyze fracture characteristics and structural complexity.

- [[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002]] analyzed Hofuf Formation samples to characterize local lithology.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013]] studied the hofuf-formation to characterize its sedimentary properties and subsurface structure.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] part of the sedimentary sequence in the eastern part of Saudi Arabia.

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] studied the Hofuf Formation to analyze fracture characteristics and structural complexity.

- [[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002]] analyzed Hofuf Formation samples to characterize local lithology.

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- studied the Hofuf Formation ([[2012-iwalewa|Iwalewa, 2012]])

- The formation consists of interbedded conglomerate and alternating red and light grey argillaceous limestone. ([[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013, p. 20]])

- The Hofuf Formation consists of limestones. ([[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018, p. 37]])

- The formation contains nearly vertical fracture sets (NW and NE) at Jabel Al-Bariqa. ([[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024, p. 7]])

- The Hofuf Formation is considered the closing unit of the Arabian Tertiary deposits. ([[2002-al-ali|Al-Ali, 2002, p. 69]])
